replication_study.hypothesis: University students who report severe psychological distress (Kessler total > 29) will have lower odds of reporting higher motivation for distance learning (online_learning_1) than students without severe distress.

data_plan.source_type: Online self-administered questionnaire distributed via Prolific Academic
data_plan.wave_or_subset: All responses after 2021-01-19 17:50:32 America/New_York (filter applied in code)
data_plan.sample_size: 766 raw rows; ~596 complete cases for focal variables

qualification.explanation: Dataset contains the same constructs (K10 distress and motivation for distance learning) collected on a new, independent sample of university students during the COVID-19 pandemic.
qualification.similarity_to_original: Uses identical K10 items (Kessler_1–10) and the same motivation item (online_learning_1) as described in original_paper.pdf and initial_details.txt.
qualification.deviation_from_original: Collected on Prolific with an international sample in Jan 2021, whereas original survey was convenience sample of Jordanian students in May 2020 (post_registration.json).

data_plan.notes: Column names follow generic pattern; dates stored as ISO strings; IDs hashed.

steps: Apply skip-rule imputation for Kessler_3 and Kessler_6
Listwise delete missing on outcome & predictor
Fit logistic regression severe_distress ~ online_learning_1 (+ controls)
planned_method.models: Binary logistic regression; sensitivity multinomial logit (4-level Kessler_categories)
planned_method.tools_software: R 4.3 with packages rio, dplyr, mlogit, rmarkdown, knitr, ggplot2, openssl
planned_method.inference_criteria: Replicate if OR < 1 and 95% CI excludes 1 (two-sided α = 0.05)

codebase.notes: Rmd reads data from /workspace/replication_data inside container

analysis.comparison_metrics: Odds ratio and 95% CI for association between severe_distress and online_learning_1
